verses.text: The bones of the dead shall be exposed before the idols they worshipped, and the survivors shall choose death over life. Even the grave is dishonoured. So bitter is the judgment that the living envy the dead.

The bones of the dead shall be exposed before the idols they worshipped, and the survivors shall choose death over life. Even the grave is dishonoured. So bitter is the judgment that the living envy the dead.

The bones of the dead shall be exposed before the idols they worshipped, and the survivors shall choose death over life. Even the grave is dishonoured. So bitter is the judgment that the living envy the dead.

Shall men fall and not rise? Even 'the stork... knoweth her appointed times,' but God's people 'know not the judgment of the LORD.' The birds shame them. Creatures keep their God-given instincts while men ignore their Maker.

Shall men fall and not rise? Even 'the stork... knoweth her appointed times,' but God's people 'know not the judgment of the LORD.' The birds shame them. Creatures keep their God-given instincts while men ignore their Maker.

Shall men fall and not rise? Even 'the stork... knoweth her appointed times,' but God's people 'know not the judgment of the LORD.' The birds shame them. Creatures keep their God-given instincts while men ignore their Maker.

Shall men fall and not rise? Even 'the stork... knoweth her appointed times,' but God's people 'know not the judgment of the LORD.' The birds shame them. Creatures keep their God-given instincts while men ignore their Maker.

How can they claim wisdom when the scribes' pens have wrought falsehood? The wise men are ashamed and trapped. Learning that twists God's word is no wisdom. Rejecting the LORD's word leaves no true wisdom at all.

How can they claim wisdom when the scribes' pens have wrought falsehood? The wise men are ashamed and trapped. Learning that twists God's word is no wisdom. Rejecting the LORD's word leaves no true wisdom at all.

From least to greatest all deal falsely, and were not even ashamed of their abominations; therefore they shall fall. Shamelessness seals their doom. A conscience that can no longer blush is near to judgment.

From least to greatest all deal falsely, and were not even ashamed of their abominations; therefore they shall fall. Shamelessness seals their doom. A conscience that can no longer blush is near to judgment.

From least to greatest all deal falsely, and were not even ashamed of their abominations; therefore they shall fall. Shamelessness seals their doom. A conscience that can no longer blush is near to judgment.
God will surely consume them; they looked for peace and health but found only trouble. False hopes collapse. The peace the false prophets promised never comes to a people in rebellion.
God will surely consume them; they looked for peace and health but found only trouble. False hopes collapse. The peace the false prophets promised never comes to a people in rebellion.
God will surely consume them; they looked for peace and health but found only trouble. False hopes collapse. The peace the false prophets promised never comes to a people in rebellion.
The enemy's horses are heard from Dan, and God sends serpents that cannot be charmed. Judgment is unstoppable. No remedy can turn back the calamity God has determined.
The enemy's horses are heard from Dan, and God sends serpents that cannot be charmed. Judgment is unstoppable. No remedy can turn back the calamity God has determined.

Jeremiah's heart faints as he hears his people's cry; 'Is there no balm in Gilead; is there no physician there?' He grieves their unhealed wound. There is a balm, but they will not come to the Physician who heals.
